title: Verification tooling that lets an agent prove editor↔export↔preview 1:1 parity
description: Research + tooling round. Make agents able to VERIFY 1:1 fidelity (not just chase it). Builds on pdoc-un3 research note. Scope bound 2026-05-23 (user): primary angle = the verification tooling/instruments an agent uses to confirm 1:1; output = research first, decide prototypes after. Current tools: structural-check (120/120, but A24-A27 ESTIMATE DOCX positions from spacing math, never renders), semantic-diff (prototype: block extraction broken, preview=null for most blocks, 1 fixture, not in CI), round-trip-check (coarse diffCount, no localization).
design: Bound decisions (grill 2026-05-23, user 'lets go build' = accept recommendations): 1) Visual Agent advisory-only, never hard-gates CI; geometry is the only hard gate; numbered block-overlay PNGs + schema {blockRef,channel,issue,severity,humanDescription}; PR+nightly cadence. 2) Block = maximal run, inter-run gap <0.6x measured line-height, reset on font-size jump >=2pt, gap self-tuned from PDF dominant line-height, recorded in metadata. 3) Non-1:1 pairing = sequence-alignment (tag+ordinal) + structural keys where available, container-first for lists/tables/callouts, unmatched->orphan verdict (no cascade). 4) Stitch per-page PDF coords into continuous document-y (prevPagesHeight + pageTop-y); validate on a 2-page fixture. 5) PRIMARY metric = relative inter-block delta, not absolute-y; absolute drift kept as non-gating diagnostic. 6) After soffice convert assert embedded PDF font names == SS4 family; fail loudly on substitution. 7) Channel tiers: editor/HTML/PDF/DOCX = full geometry gate; EPUB/Markdown = structural-only (order+presence), geometry informational; record gateLevel per channel. 8) Thresholds: geometry pass<8.5pt warn 8.5-17 fail>17 (fail blocks CI); DOCX looser fail>1.5 line-heights gateLevel reflow-sanity; SSIM warn-only floor ~0.92 never blocks CI; tolerances in one constants file. 9) Verdict record enriched {blockId,channel,dyPt,dxPt,threshold,verdict,blockType,textSnippet~40ch,astNodeId?,reason}; zero-text where editor has text -> FAIL(no-text) + SSIM fallback. 10) Self-test: known-good must PASS; known-bad(+30pt one heading) must FAIL that block + PASS elsewhere; both in CI. 11) T2 first channel = HTML + hard fixture (list+table+callout); DOCX deferred to T4. 12) LibreOffice accepted as DOCX oracle (Word no Linux build); quirk list documented in T6. 13) Clean replacement of semantic-diff: delete harvestBlocks + CDN docx-preview/epub.js path; new output namespace .papir-check/geometry/; remove stale result-*.json.

Research methods to push editor↔export parity past structural-100 — semantic visual diffing, render-engine harmonization, ground-truth fixtures, machine-readable parity benchmarks
description:
---

## Bound decisions (questionnaire 2026-05-20-parity-research, user "follow recommendations")

1. **Research lanes**: Semantic visual diff + Round-trip parity + Prototype-driven (lit/tool survey folded into the research note itself)
2. **Swing size**: Research note + 1-2 prototype patches + harness extensions
3. **Most-painful divergence to attack**: DOCX synthesized italic/bold (escape docx@9.6.1 public-API limit via direct OPC manipulation)
4. **Benchmark prior art**: Pandoc + Typst (covered in research note)
5. **Research-vs-prototype balance**: 50/50
6. **Success criteria**: (a) new structural-check assertion class for sub-em + (b) working DOCX italic/bold patch + (c) research note + (d) prioritized backlog
7. **New test classes**: Semantic visual diff + Round-trip parity hardening + Cross-renderer fidelity (cross-renderer downgraded to backlog — needs Linux/Windows runners we lack today)
8. **Definition of "closer"**: Sub-em layout equivalence (every block within ±1 line-height of editor placement)
9. **No additional lanes named**

build
review
pre-flight
1:1 fidelity — editor canvas matches every export (DOCX, EPUB, PDF, HTML, TUI) and the four preview panels: same font, same spacing model, same visual layout end to end
description:
---

## Bound decisions (grill 2026-05-20-render-parity-grill, user "follow recommendations")

1. **Font weights**: Regular + Italic + Bold + BoldItalic (4 TTFs, ~1.2MB)
2. **DOCX embed**: plain `.ttf` in `word/fonts/` (SIL OFL — no obfuscation needed)
3. **EPUB font shipping**: always bundle in `OPS/fonts/`
4. **HTML font shipping**: base64 inline (single-file portable)
5. **Heading scale**: keep 28/22/18/11pt (editor current)
6. **Spacing model**: global lock 12pt before / 0pt after / 1.55 line-height. Per-channel overrides only for list-item spacing + table cells.
7. **Structural assertions**: structural fidelity only (no line counts — that is the pixelmatch noise floor in disguise)
8. **font-display**: `swap` (FOUT > FOIT for portable docs)
9. **Code blocks**: keep monospace exception (JetBrains Mono in PDF, monospace elsewhere — serif code is unreadable)
10. **TUI parity**: structural + palette + 80-col flow. Font equivalence out of scope by definition.
11. **TTF vendoring**: `apps/editor/public/fonts/` (zero install-time risk, deterministic, no CDN dep)
12. **structural-check cadence**: CI only initially, local `pnpm check:structural` available

Review: design-tokens-spec pre-flight
notes:
VERDICT (revised): approved.

All 4 issues fixed:
MAJOR 1: C1 Zod schema rewritten as z.discriminatedUnion across family — callout(tone × emphasis), action(priority × size), section(density), code(theme × density). Validates all 21 catalog rows.
MINOR 2: D2 acceptance reworded to 're-export from @portable-doc/variants' (the canonical source-of-truth) per bound decision 7. The contradiction was removed from C4's open-questions list.
MINOR 3: C3 example backend lifted renderBlock/renderInline to standalone functions; pdfBackend literal references them by name instead of via 'this.renderBlock!'. Strict-TS-safe; type assertions removed.
MINOR 4: C2 added the per-family CSS class shapes (paper-action-primary-large, paper-section-compact, paper-code-dark-normal) so readers don't have to infer them.

All 4 specs pass paperflow-validate post-fix. Pre-flight is ship-ready for the next /paperflow:plan to materialize D1-D10 build tasks.

Publish PortableDoc as an open design-token spec — variant catalog as JSON schema with reference renders for CSS, OOXML styles.xml, EPUB CSS, and email; backend interface for custom render targets in ~100 LOC
description:
Publish PortableDoc as an open design-token spec — variant catalog as JSON/Zod schema with reference renders for CSS, OOXML styles.xml seed, EPUB CSS, and email inline styles; backend interface for custom render targets in ~100 LOC.

BOUND DECISIONS (no grill — momentum mode, recommendations accepted):
 1. Package name: @portable-doc/design-tokens (published from packages/design-tokens/)
 2. Schema definition: Zod (consistent with the envelope schema from Goal B); JSON Schema is a derived export via zod-to-json-schema
 3. Versioning: SemVer of the package; major bumps when variant axes (tone/emphasis/family) change shape
 4. Reference renders shipped: CSS, OOXML styles.xml seed, EPUB CSS, email inline-styles
 5. Backend interface: TypeScript interface PortableDocBackend with adapter pattern; example custom backend (PDF stub) in ~100 LOC
 6. Spec home: ~/docs/paperflow/specs/ for now; public site URL is post-v1
 7. Catalog source of truth: packages/variants stays canonical; @portable-doc/design-tokens is a publishable view of it (re-exports + the schema + renderers)
 8. License: MIT (matches the rest of the monorepo)

build
pre-flight
Round-trip-safe exports — every Papir export embeds the PortableDoc AST so reimporting any output restores the exact editor state
description:
Round-trip-safe exports — every Papir export embeds the PortableDoc AST so reimporting any output restores the exact editor state.

BOUND DECISIONS (grill resolved 2026-05-19 — recommendations accepted as-is):
 1. Envelope shape: wrapped — { version, exporter, exported_at, doc_uuid, ast }
 2. Doc UUID: required, generated on first export, stored in editor state
 3. Compression: gzip+base64 for .md only; raw JSON for .docx/.html/.epub
 4. Validation: zod at the import boundary; errors are programmable
 5. Conflict resolution: embedded-AST wins for structure; host edits surface as 'imported' margin-noted blocks (reuse A10 margin notes)
 6. Embedding mode: always-on (opt-in breaks the trust pitch)
 7. .pages support: deferred (no write path exists for the closed format)
 8. Pre-feature exports: hybrid — detect no-envelope → 'Import as new' button → soft fallback to reverse-parse

title: Review: multi-format-export pre-flight
notes:
VERDICT (revised): approved.

The two major issues were fixed:
- EPUB CSS class naming aligned across P2, P4, P5 to P3's canonical paper-* kebab-case form (pdoc-cbr, pdoc-4wn, pdoc-1e9 reopened, edited, reclosed).
- EPUB stylesheet path corrected: P2 OEBPS/styles.css → OPS/styles/paper.css. Other OEBPS strings also removed.

Verification (post-fix):
- grep 'pd-callout|pd-code|pd-action|pd-section|pd-chip|OEBPS/' P2 → 0
- grep 'pd-' P4 → 0
- grep 'pf-callout|pf-code|pf-action|pf-section|pf-chip' P5 → 0
- paperflow-validate P2/P3/P4/P5 all ok:true (zero Mermaid failures across 11 blocks)
- P2 now contains illustrative Word style names (CalloutSuccessSubtle, ActionPrimaryMedium, SectionCompact, CodeDarkNormal) for spot-check parity with P3.

Minor issues 3, 4, 5, 6 from the original verdict are NOT addressed in this fix (P6 missing docs-page + changelog tasks; P6 dropped P2's UUID-strategy open question; P2 illustrative styles partially addressed via the spot-check anchor). These are deferred to the build-phase /paperflow:plan invocation that materializes B1-B10. Logged here for that planner to pick up.

review
build
pre-flight
Export Papir docs to Word, Pages, Google Docs, and EPUB with style fidelity
description:
Export Papir docs to Word, Pages, Google Docs, and EPUB with style fidelity.

BOUND DECISIONS (grill resolved 2026-05-18 — recommendations accepted as-is):
 1. Mechanism: pandoc-wasm in-browser
 2. Format set v1: DOCX + EPUB (strict)
 3. Round-trip: one-way only for v1
 4. EPUB version: EPUB 3 with EPUB 2 backwards-compatible structure (nav.xhtml + ncx.xml)
 5. Converter location: browser-first (in-Papir export button), CLI second-class
 6. reference.docx authoring: hybrid — code generates styles.xml from variant mapping; designer polishes in Word
 7. Variant fidelity: exact 1:1 mapping (every variant → named Word style)
 8. Edge case default: warning callout in exported doc (no silent drop, no abort)
 9. Export UI: single export button with format dropdown
10. Async vs sync: sync (wasm is fast)
11. Test fixture: one exhaustive + per-variant regressions (both)
12. CI: parse-and-assert primary; visual diff as --visual flag
